metadata.description: PAEC RAMP corpus v2.3 — Bug fixes: engine plural support (engines?), runway extraction isolation, Sierra phase detection tightened. New error type: runway_designator_omitted. 5 more patterns migrated to CHECKS (enginePattern, runwayPattern, holdShortEquivalentsPattern, chocksConfirmationPattern, runwayDesignatorPattern). ~26 new entries: engine plural, runway designator, hold-short variants. Total 17 dynamic CHECKS keys.

corpus.atc: Philippine Airlines one zero five, approved to push, Sierra one, facing east, runway zero six left.
corpus.pilot: Yep, Sierra one, facing east, runway zero six left. Philippine Airlines one zero five.
corpus.explanation: 'Yep' is non-standard pilot phraseology in ATC communications per ICAO Doc 9432.

corpus.atc: Cebu Pacific six three eight, taxi november five, hold short golf one four.
corpus.pilot: Gotcha, november five, hold short golf one four. Cebu Pacific six three eight.
corpus.explanation: 'Gotcha' is casual, non-standard pilot phraseology. Use standard readback phraseology per ICAO Doc 9432.

corpus.atc: Cebu Pacific three zero five, continue tow bay one one two.
corpus.pilot: Sure sir, continue tow bay one one two. Cebu Pacific three zero five.
corpus.explanation: 'Sure sir' is informal and non-standard. Honorifics and casual affirmatives should not appear in readbacks per ICAO Doc 9432.

corpus.atc: Philippine Airlines two zero one, idle power approved.
corpus.pilot: Copy that sir, idle power approved. Philippine Airlines two zero one.
corpus.explanation: 'Copy that sir' includes informal honorific. Standard phrasing is 'Idle power approved' without sir per ICAO Doc 9432.

corpus.atc: Royal Brunei two three one, approved to push, Sierra two, facing north, runway two four.
corpus.pilot: Ok sir, push Sierra two, facing north, runway two four. Royal Brunei two three one.
corpus.explanation: 'Ok sir' is non-standard in aviation communications. ICAO phraseology does not include honorifics per ICAO Doc 9432.

corpus.atc: Cathay Pacific eight eight nine, hold short golf one four. Contact ground one two one point nine.
corpus.pilot: Gotcha, hold short golf one four, ground one two one point nine. Cathay Pacific eight eight nine.
corpus.explanation: 'Gotcha' is non-standard pilot phraseology. Standard readback should omit casual acknowledgments per ICAO Doc 9432.

corpus.atc: Philippine Airlines one zero five, engine start approved.
corpus.pilot: No problem, engine start approved. Philippine Airlines one zero five.
corpus.explanation: 'No problem' is non-standard phraseology in ATC communications. Standard acknowledgment is required per ICAO Doc 9432.

corpus.atc: Cebu Pacific five zero five, approved to push Sierra five, facing south, runway zero six right.
corpus.pilot: Yeah, pushback Sierra five, facing south, runway zero six right. Cebu Pacific five zero five.
corpus.explanation: 'Yeah' is informal and non-standard in ATC communications. Standard readback per ICAO Doc 9432 should be used.

corpus.atc: Philippine Airlines two one two, contact ground one two one decimal nine.
corpus.pilot: Sure ma, ground one two one decimal nine. Philippine Airlines two one two.
corpus.explanation: 'Sure ma' is informal and contains a non-standard address form. Frequency handoff should use standard phraseology per ICAO Doc 9432.

corpus.atc: Cebu Pacific nine two four, approved to push, Sierra four, facing east, runway zero six left.
corpus.pilot: Ok ma, push Sierra four, facing east, runway zero six left. Cebu Pacific nine two four.
corpus.explanation: 'Ok ma' is informal and non-standard. ICAO phraseology does not include casual honorifics per ICAO Doc 9432.

corpus.explanation: Pilot said 'Confirmed' without explicitly mentioning brake status. ATC expects confirmation that parking brakes are set.

corpus.atc: Philippine Airlines one zero five, parking brake set, advise ready.
corpus.pilot: Will advise when ready. Philippine Airlines one zero five.
corpus.explanation: Pilot responded without confirming parking brakes were set. ATC expects explicit brake confirmation before ground crew proceeds.

corpus.explanation: ATC instructed to set parking brake; pilot responded with status only — no brake confirmation given.

corpus.atc: Cathay Pacific eight eight nine, confirm parking brakes set.
corpus.pilot: Cathay Pacific eight eight nine, we are parked.
corpus.explanation: Pilot confirmed parked position but did not confirm parking brakes set per ATC instruction.

corpus.atc: Cebu Pacific three zero five, confirm brakes set, chocks in.
corpus.pilot: Roger, Cebu Pacific three zero five.
corpus.explanation: ATC requested brake and chocks confirmation; pilot only said 'Roger' — no explicit brake or chocks confirmation.

corpus.explanation: Towing instructions must be read back in full. 'Roger' means controller cannot verify crew received the correct bay number.

corpus.explanation: 'Ok' is both non-standard and insufficient for a pushback clearance. Full readback required per ICAO Doc 4444 §12.3.1.
